content.text:
Deep Learning
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
This module provides knowledge on machine learning as a very important pillar of artificial intelligence, with neural networks as a specific case.
Course Content (21h)
Chapter 1 – Introduction to Deep Learning
Definition, origin, and links with machine learning
Neural networks: general principle
Importance of massive data and parallel computing (GPU, TPUs)
Use cases (vision, NLP, audio, time series)
Chapter 2 – Artificial Neural Networks (ANN)
Formal neuron, activation functions (ReLU, sigmoid, tanh)
Forward propagation and backpropagation
Cost function and gradient descent (SGD, Adam)
Overfitting and regularisation techniques: dropout, early stopping, L2
Chapter 3 – Practical Tools and Frameworks
Introduction to TensorFlow and Keras
Data manipulation with NumPy and Pandas
Creating, training, and evaluating a simple ANN model
Saving, loading, and visualising models
Chapter 4 – Convolutional Networks (CNN)
CNN architecture: conv, pooling, fully connected layers
Dimensionality reduction, local pattern detection
Application to image classification (MNIST, CIFAR-10)
Techniques: data augmentation, batch normalisation
Chapter 5 – Recurrent Networks (RNN) and Sequential Models
Simple RNNs and limitations (vanishing gradient)
LSTM and GRU
Application to time series and NLP
Sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) and attentional models
Chapter 6 – Advanced Models and Recent Trends
Auto-encoders: dimensionality reduction, denoising
GAN (Generative Adversarial Networks): principles, applications
Introduction to Transformers: architecture and interest (vision, NLP)
Overview of pre-trained models (BERT, GPT, ResNet)
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
Gérard Dreyfus et al. (2004).
Réseaux de neurones, Méthodologies et applications
. 2nd edition, Eyrolles.
Golovko, V. A. (2017). Deep learning: an overview and main paradigms.
Optical memory and neural networks
, 26, 1-17.
David Rousseau,
L'apprentissage machine
. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=utaKQ-obVR8
